name: creating-issues
description: Issue creation expertise and convention enforcement. Auto-invokes when creating issues, writing issue descriptions, asking about issue best practices, or needing help with issue titles. Validates naming conventions, suggests labels, and ensures proper metadata.
capabilities: Validates issue titles follow naming conventions without type prefixes
Suggests appropriate type, priority, and scope labels for issues
Auto-detects scope labels from branch names and project context
Provides bug report, feature request, and standard issue templates
Enforces required metadata before issue creation
Creates issues with proper labels using GitHub CLI
author: C0ntr0lledCha0s
securitySummary: This skill provides GitHub issue creation assistance with Python helper scripts. All static findings are false positives: the scanner misidentified markdown documentation formatting (backticks) and placeholder text as security issues. Legitimate subprocess calls invoke only trusted GitHub CLI tools with hardcoded arguments. The skill is safe for publication.
valueStatement: Creating GitHub issues with proper conventions takes time and attention to detail. This skill validates issue titles, suggests appropriate labels, and ensures every issue includes required metadata like scope labels and priority levels.
userTitle: Create well-formed GitHub issues with proper labels
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI (gh) to be installed and authenticated
Cannot create issues without proper scope label configuration
Does not modify existing issues, only creates new ones
Depends on project having label taxonomy defined

useCases.title: Standardize issue creation
useCases.description: Ensure all contributors create issues with consistent labels, titles, and metadata structure.

useCases.title: Track work by scope
useCases.description: Automatically detect component scope from branch names and add proper scope labels to issues.

useCases.title: Review issue quality
useCases.description: Validate that proposed issue titles follow conventions and suggest improvements before creation.

promptTemplates.title: Quick issue creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an issue for fixing the login timeout bug in the authentication module

promptTemplates.title: With context
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an issue for adding dark mode support based on the current feature branch

promptTemplates.title: With validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Is this a good issue title: [BUG] Fix the crash when saving files? If not, suggest a better one

promptTemplates.title: Bulk creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create issues for implementing user authentication with proper labels and scope for the auth component

bestPractices: Always include scope labels to enable context-aware filtering and commit integration
Use imperative mood in titles without type prefixes like [BUG] or [FEATURE]
Define acceptance criteria before creating issues to clarify completion requirements
Ask clarifying questions about project board, milestones, and relationships when creating multiple issues
antiPatterns: Using type prefixes in titles (e.g., [BUG] Login fails) instead of relying on labels
Creating issues without scope labels that identify the affected component
Skipping acceptance criteria which leads to unclear completion requirements
Using task lists instead of GraphQL parent-child relationships for sub-issues

faq.question: What GitHub CLI permissions are required?
faq.answer: Requires repo scope for issue creation and label management. Install via gh auth login.

faq.question: How many labels are required per issue?
faq.answer: Exactly three: one type (bug, feature, enhancement), one priority (high, medium, low), and one scope.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with GitHub Enterprise?
faq.answer: Yes, works with any GitHub instance where gh CLI is authenticated including Enterprise Server.

faq.question: Does this skill store or transmit my data?
faq.answer: No. All processing is local. Scripts only invoke gh CLI commands to interact with GitHub.

faq.question: What happens if scope cannot be auto-detected?
faq.answer: The skill prompts you to manually select a scope from available project scopes before creating.

faq.question: How is this different from GitHub issue templates?
faq.answer: This skill provides real-time validation and suggestions during creation, while GitHub templates only define body formats.
